summary: Rachel Torres (Senior Account Manager) and Sofia Petrov (Senior Engineer) at Aegis held a pre-deployment discovery call with Derek Owens at Crestline to discuss adding the Aegis Identity module to their existing Protect and Detect deployment. Derek's organization has ~340 users on Entra ID with a mixed MFA environment and needs to address privileged access management gaps flagged in a February audit. Sofia outlined a three-phase deployment approach covering directory sync, MFA policy migration, and PAM, with an end-of-July target that is feasible given Crestline's environment. Derek also expressed interest in Aegis Comply v2 as a future add-on, which Rachel noted would benefit from the audit data generated during the Identity deployment.
actionItems: Sofia Petrov: Prepare and send deployment plan document by end of day Monday, including three-phase timeline, Entra ID and ServiceNow integration checklist, MFA phased migration framework, and just-in-time PAM overview
Rachel Torres: Send calendar invite with options for a kickoff call early next week
Derek Owens: Share deployment plan document with manager for review before the kickoff call
Derek Owens: Bring Aegis Comply v2 SOC 2 reporting capabilities to the attention of the Chief Compliance Officer

keyMoments.text: Derek reveals audit flagged gaps in privileged access management and MFA coverage, driving urgency for Identity deployment
keyMoments.speaker: Derek Owens

keyMoments.text: Sofia confirms phased MFA rollout is supported, eliminating the need for a disruptive big-bang cutover and alleviating Derek's concern about advisor pushback
keyMoments.speaker: Sofia Petrov

keyMoments.text: Derek highlights terminated employees retaining admin access too long due to manual offboarding, reinforcing the PAM and automated deprovisioning need
keyMoments.speaker: Derek Owens

keyMoments.text: Derek raises interest in Aegis Comply v2 after seeing the product announcement, opening a potential upsell opportunity
keyMoments.speaker: Derek Owens
